title: Intensely Citrusy Ethiopian Kabocha Dulet
description: A chewy and citrusy Ethiopian dish featuring kabocha, perfect for a date night, with an al-dente finish and a dash of friendly spices.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cubed kabocha squash and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the berbere spice, lemon juice, orange juice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the squash is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the dulet hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Traditionally, dulet is served with injera bread, but it can also be served with rice or other grains.
